inspirations.religion: Mostly set up like the Greek pantheon but with a few twists. The gods are more active and present in the world, but they don't necessarily intervene directly in mortal affairs. They have specific domains and philosophies that influence their followers, but they more often than not don't demand worship or obedience. Names are heavily influenced by Gaulic and Saxon names.
inspirations.cities: Each major city should have a relatively distinct climate and culture inspired by a mix of real-world cultures but avoid the obvious ones. They need defined demographics, governments, economies, and something that sets them apart.
inspirations.continents: Two continents are known: Kaemahz and Yetgrese. Other continents may exist but are speculative — only these two have any established information. Kaemahz is the primary fantasy-familiar continent; Yetgrese is deliberately unusual with no standard city structures.

continents.name: Kaemahz

calendar_system.notes: The 8 day weeks leave 4 days leftover, which is called the Spring Festival halfweek. This is regionally celebrated as a time of renewal and community gathering, with various local traditions. The Spring Festival halfweek is not considered part of any season, but rather a transition period between Winter and Spring.

continents.description: Large continental landmass divided into seven regions. The primary fantasy-familiar continent of the known world — fulfills traditional tropes of kingdoms, trade routes, and established civilizations. Dragonborn presence is strong throughout, particularly concentrated in the southern regions.

notable_features.name: Inkaba
notable_features.notes: Active but not constantly erupting. Located roughly in the center of Kaemahz, east of Cindermarch. Visible from the fortress on clear days. Name means 'navel' (Zulu origin) — implies it is the origin point of the continent. Contributes to Cindermarch's weather pattern: snow to the north, clear to the south.

continents.name: Yetgrese
continents.description: Second continent. 2-3 weeks sailing from Kaemahz's western coast; under 2 weeks with magical propulsion if crossing goes smoothly.
continents.climate: Variable regionally. Interior sea climate likely distinct from exterior coasts due to geological shelter.

geography.shape: Crescent — massive partial crater or caldera, eroded asymmetrically over millennia
geography.western: Massive, mountainous, intact. Tallest and most dramatic terrain. Volcanic or meteoric origin — meteoric preferred, TBD.
geography.eastern: Partially eroded and broken. Lower elevation. Where the old crater wall wore away.
geography.gap: Open waterway where the eastern wall eroded through — connects outer ocean to interior sea.
geography.interior_sea: Massively deep. Geologically sheltered and isolated for millennia. Likely contains unique fauna. Mostly cliffed borders with no beaches — cliffs grow dramatically taller moving west.
geography.archipelago: Complex island chain in the interior where the eroded eastern face crumbled into the water over millennia. Remnants of the old crater wall.
geography.target_city: On the eastern face of the main continent, slightly north of the waterway gap, facing outward toward the open sea.

demographics.notes: Humans are 15-20% of population at most. Several familiar races present but as less common or divergent subtypes — not standard Kaemahz versions. Full demographic composition still being established. Do not default to human NPCs.

continents.notes: Dangerous crossing — weather, distance, and navigation all factors. Difficult enough that most Kaemahz residents have never met anyone from there. Occasional traders make the crossing. Target city is trade-accessible but not frequently visited — arrival is noteworthy but not unprecedented.

name: Fynesmarch
climate: Temperate maritime overall, distinct microclimates city to city.
shape_note: Wide and shallow — twice as far east-west as north-south.
naming_conventions: Literal and descriptive. Place names directly reflect observable features — Cindermarch is a fortress (march) near ashen terrain from the Inkaba volcano. This pattern should guide generation of new place names consistently.
notes: Primary campaign region. Cindermarch on its northeastern border served as a fortress during past conflict with Fol — that conflict has ended. Cindermarch is now both a fortress and a functioning city.

cities.name: Ravenquay
cities.description: River delta. Dawn Blades home base.
cities.notes: 1 day sail south of Trevash, 3 days sail south of Khemrise.

cities.name: Trevash
cities.notes: 2 days sail south of Khemrise, 1 day sail north of Ravenquay.

cities.name: Khemrise
cities.description: Major coastal city, vineyards. Pivot point where the coast turns north-south.
cities.notes: 2 days sail north of Trevash, 3 days sail north of Ravenquay.

cities.name: Millhaven
cities.notes: 2 days northeast of Khemrise, 1 day south of Cindermarch, 4 days north of Ravenquay.

cities.name: Argentveil
cities.description: Southern coast after eastward bend. Warmest, most cosmopolitan.

cities.name: Cindermarch
cities.description: Military fortress and city on the Fol border.
cities.notes: 1 day north of Millhaven, 3 days northeast of Khemrise, 4-5 days ride from Ravenquay.

cities.name: Valdmere
cities.description: Northwest coast, faces south. Frontier port.

cities.name: Halvenmoren
cities.description: Border town at the mouth of Dunric Pass.

wilderness.name: Crossroads Rest
wilderness.description: Waystation on main road. 2 days north of Ravenquay toward Millhaven.

wilderness.name: Verath Ohn
wilderness.description: Off southern coast, in the water.

name: Fol
culture: Tribal and shamanistic. Ironworking is culturally central — not merely a trade skill but an identity, bound up in spiritual practice. Shamanism is the primary spiritual framework. No merchant-council structure, no capital.

known: Veratha — known as the Firstwalkers. The name implies a belief that they were the first people to walk the land, potentially predating other civilizations on Kaemahz.
tribes.notes: Avoid generating more V-names for new tribes in this region.

sub_regions.name: Northern Cliffs
sub_regions.description: Cliff-faced coastline, no sea access, harsh winters, no sailing culture.

notes: Past conflict with Fynesmarch has ended. Cindermarch fortress exists due to historical raids, not active conflict.
shape_notes: Top point (20,40) — furthest north. Top left coast (20,40) to (0,20). Top right coast (20,40) to (40,20). Bottom left border (0,20) to (8,0). Bottom right border (40,20) to (32,0). Bottom edge (8,0) to (32,0) — Fynesmarch border.

locations.name: Cindermarch
locations.description: Bottom center on border line. Military fortress, Fynesmarch side.

locations.name: Northern Rock Waystation
locations.description: Rock overhang shelter. Firstwalker songs carved in hearthstone.

locations.name: Vethara Winter Camp
locations.description: Northeast of Cindermarch. Firstwalker winter camp.

locations.name: Greyspine Passage
locations.description: Narrow mountain passage. Only winter route through the ridge.

locations.name: Observatory
locations.description: Ancient pre-settlement structure. Mast's research base.

name: Torwald
culture: Anglo-Saxon cultural feel. Considers itself the keeper of original culture — both its own and what Aranwald set aside. Slightly smug about this. Never occupied by Solvale.
relationship_with_aranwald: Familial and good overall, with a persistent tension. Torwald finds Aranwald's embrace of Solvaran aesthetics gauche — sees it as celebrating the style of an occupier. Torwald views itself as the keeper of what both kingdoms once were; Aranwald regards this position as provincial.
history: Once a single kingdom with what is now Aranwald. The Solvale Empire occupied the southern half for approximately 200 years, effectively splitting the territory. A prince eventually recaptured the occupied territory and renamed it Aranwald, formalizing the split. A sibling on the Torwald side was reportedly indignant about not being the one to recapture it — the kingdoms separated formally but retained familial connection. See Aranwald for the full account.

name: Aranwald
culture: Persian-Anglo fusion. Occupied by the Solvale Empire for approximately 200 years. A prince recaptured it and coined the name deliberately — kept 'wald' for continuity with the land, replaced 'Tor' with 'Aran' (Persian: peaceful/noble). Founding philosophy: 'we didn't just conquer the land, we conquered everything.' Solvaran artifacts, architecture, and cultural elements are high-status symbols — trophies of strength, not shameful remnants. Solvaran scholars and craftsmen increasingly emigrating here as the empire destabilizes.
solvaran_cultural_inheritance: The occupation left a deep imprint that Aranwald has deliberately reclaimed rather than erased: domed buildings and arched courtyards, elaborate geometric gardens, rich textiles and ornate metalwork, a strong poetry and oral tradition, possible fire worship or fire-adjacent religious practice, a taste for court intrigue, and a sophisticated bureaucratic tradition. These elements are visible throughout Feldtazur's architecture and the aristocratic culture of the region.
relationship_with_torwald: Familial and good overall. Aranwald regards Torwald's insistence on pre-Solvaran tradition as provincial — clinging to what was rather than mastering what is. Both views are stated with family fondness rather than hostility.
split_history: Torwald and Aranwald were once a single kingdom. The Solvare Empire's occupation of the southern half lasted approximately 200 years before a prince recaptured the territory and renamed it Aranwald, breaking formal unity permanently. A sibling on the Torwald side was reportedly indignant about not being the hero of that moment. The kingdoms split but retained familial connection. The naming of Aranwald was a deliberate philosophical act, not merely a geographic one.
notes: Capital name Feldtazur coined by the prince — Anglo 'feld' + Persian-derived 'tazur'.

name: Orvath
culture: Primarily elven population. Mongolian-inspired — what this means in practice is TBD (nomadic heritage expressed in a settled city? clan-based social structure? horse culture adapted to desert context?). Question-free banking is the defining feature to outsiders.
banking: Anonymous deposits, no extradition, no questions asked — by design, not accident. The most secure financial institution on Kaemahz. How this status was established and maintained is TBD, but the implication is either significant political neutrality, deep historical precedent, or a power arrangement that makes interference more costly than cooperation.
origin_notes: How a question-free banking haven came to exist in the middle of a high desert is TBD — worth developing as a campaign or world-building element.
notes: Contains only one city, also called Orvath. 6 days north/northeast of Argentveil overland.

name: Birindal
culture: Two distinct communities maintain separate but cooperative identities. Coastal (Drinaell): Celtic/Nordic seafaring culture — merchant marine and raiding heritage, community governance by harbor council or similar. Mountain (Brakvunder): Dwarven stronghold culture — deep craft tradition, independent governance, minimal interest in maritime affairs.
governance: No capital because the coastal and mountain communities govern themselves independently. No central authority both sides would accept — and neither has pushed for one. The arrangement works because their needs are complementary rather than competitive.
drinaell_brakvunder_relationship: Maritime trade partnership. Brakvunder produces metal, stone, and finished craft goods; Drinaell moves them. Drinaell provides sea access and external trade networks; Brakvunder provides inland security and resources. How Drinaell became the dominant maritime force on Kaemahz is TBD — likely geography, shipbuilding tradition, and absence of strong competition from other regions.

name: Solvale
climate: Warm to tropical, North African/Moroccan feel.
culture: Once the heart of the Solvale Empire — still uses the imperial name. People are called Solvaran. At its height: architecturally stunning, culturally sophisticated, center of learning and trade. Cultural exports — art, poetry, bureaucratic method, architectural style — shaped Aranwald for generations of occupation.
collapse: The empire extracted Aranwald's resources for approximately 200 years, then lost it. The entire economic engine collapsed without that resource base. Dictatorial leadership that had built the empire proved unable to adapt — the same structures that enabled expansion prevented recovery.
current_state: Yshavar is now a city built for more people than inhabit it. Public buildings maintained by habit more than necessity. The grandeur is still visible — the bones of empire are impressive — but the scale no longer matches the population.
notes: Southernmost region of Kaemahz. A recovery path exists but is politically nearly impossible without outside intervention or significant leadership change. Potential campaign arc.
